{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 祥雲寺 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]
東戸祭1-1-16 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|34|16|N|139|53|1|E}}| |山号 = 戸祭山 |宗派 = [[曹洞宗]] |本尊 = |創建年 = |開基 = 良訓和尚（開山） |正式名 = 戸祭山 祥雲寺 |別称 = |札所等 = （祥雲寺観音霊場あり） |文化財 = [[しだれ桜]]（県指定[[天然記念物]]） |}} '''祥雲寺'''（しょううんじ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]東戸祭一丁目にある[[曹洞宗]]の[[寺院]]。山号は戸祭山。境内には祥雲寺観音霊場があり、当寺境内内で[[西国三十三ヶ所観音霊場]]の観音巡りができる。 == 境内 == * [[仏堂|本堂]] * 庫院 * [[山門]] * 伝燈閣 * 道正観音 * 羅漢渓 * 祥雲寺観音霊場（[[西国三十三ヶ所観音霊場]]） * 墓地 ** 戸祭備中守高定 墓碑 – 戸祭高定は[[宇都宮氏]]第7代当主[[宇都宮景綱]]（[[1235年]]～[[1298年]]）の弟である[[宇都宮盛綱]]の子[[宇都宮泰貞]]の後裔で、この地に戸祭城を築城したとされる。 * その他 ** 祥雲寺境内内古墳（あまし塚古墳） ** [[しだれ桜]] ** 梅林 == 文化財 == * しだれ桜 ** [[江戸時代]]の[[明暦]]年間（[[1655年]]～[[1658年]]）に植えられたとされる古木で、樹齢300年といわれる。高さ約20m太さ5m、枝は東西約20m、南北約30mに広がる。 :: [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、[[栃木県]]より[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。 == アクセス == ;バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]　[[戸祭台団地|戸祭台]]循環線、西塙田経由戸祭・細谷行き乗車10分、「[[宇都宮市立昭和小学校|昭和小学校]]前」下車徒歩3分。 ;徒歩 * [[東武宇都宮駅]]から ** 徒歩20分 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[宇都宮氏]] * [[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]] * [[戸祭山]] * [[曹洞宗]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しよううんし}} [[Category:栃木県の寺]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 祥雲寺 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]
東戸祭1-1-16 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|34|16|N|139|53|1|E}}| |山号 = 戸祭山 |宗派 = [[曹洞宗]] |本尊 = |創建年 = |開基 = 良訓和尚（開山） |正式名 = 戸祭山 祥雲寺 |別称 = |札所等 = （祥雲寺観音霊場あり） |文化財 = [[しだれ桜]]（県指定[[天然記念物]]） |}} '''祥雲寺'''（しょううんじ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]東戸祭一丁目にある[[曹洞宗]]の[[寺院]]。山号は戸祭山。境内には祥雲寺観音霊場があり、当寺境内内で[[西国三十三ヶ所観音霊場]]の観音巡りができる。 == 境内 == * [[仏堂|本堂]] * 庫院 * [[山門]] * 伝燈閣 * 道正観音 * 羅漢渓 * 祥雲寺観音霊場（[[西国三十三ヶ所観音霊場]]） * 墓地 ** 戸祭備中守高定 墓碑 – 戸祭高定は[[宇都宮氏]]第7代当主[[宇都宮景綱]]（[[1235年]]～[[1298年]]）の弟である[[宇都宮盛綱]]の子[[宇都宮泰貞]]の後裔で、この地に戸祭城を築城したとされる。 * その他 ** 祥雲寺境内内古墳（あまし塚古墳） ** [[しだれ桜]] ** 梅林 == 文化財 == * しだれ桜 ** [[江戸時代]]の[[明暦]]年間（[[1655年]]～[[1658年]]）に植えられたとされる古木で、樹齢300年といわれる。高さ約20m太さ5m、枝は東西約20m、南北約30mに広がる。 :: [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、[[栃木県]]より[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。 == アクセス == ;バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]　[[戸祭台団地|戸祭台]]循環線、西塙田経由戸祭・細谷行き乗車10分、「[[宇都宮市立昭和小学校|昭和小学校]]前」下車徒歩3分。 ;徒歩 * [[東武宇都宮駅]]から ** 徒歩20分 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[宇都宮氏]] * [[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]] * [[戸祭山]] * [[曹洞宗]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しよううんし}} [[Category:栃木県の寺]] [[Category:栃木県の建築物・観光名所]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]

[[戸祭|東戸祭1-1-16]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|34|16|N|139|53|1|E}}| |山号 = [[戸祭山]] |宗派 = [[曹洞宗]] |本尊 = |創建年 = |開基 = 良訓和尚（開山） |正式名 = [[戸祭山]] 祥雲寺 |別称 = |札所等 = （祥雲寺観音霊場あり） |文化財 = [[しだれ桜]]（県指定[[天然記念物]]） |}} '''祥雲寺'''（しょううんじ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]][[戸祭|東戸祭一丁目]]にある[[曹洞宗]]の[[寺院]]である。山号は[[戸祭山]]。境内には祥雲寺観音霊場があり、当寺境内内で[[西国三十三所|西国三十三所観音霊場]]の観音巡りができる。 == 境内 == * [[仏堂|本堂]] * 庫院 * [[山門]] * 伝燈閣 * 道正観音 * 羅漢渓 * 祥雲寺観音霊場（[[西国三十三所]]） * 墓地 ** 戸祭備中守高定 墓碑 – 戸祭高定は[[宇都宮氏]]第7代当主[[宇都宮景綱]]（[[1235年]]～[[1298年]]）の弟である[[宇都宮盛綱]]の子[[宇都宮泰貞]]の後裔で、この地に戸祭城を築城したとされる。 * その他 ** 祥雲寺境内内古墳（あまし塚古墳） ** [[しだれ桜]] ** 梅林 == 文化財 == * しだれ桜 ** [[江戸時代]]の[[明暦]]年間（[[1655年]]～[[1658年]]）に植えられたとされる古木で、樹齢300年といわれる。高さ約20m太さ5m、枝は東西約20m、南北約30mに広がる。 :: [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、[[栃木県]]より[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。 == アクセス == ;バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]][[宇都宮駅バスのりば|西口]]から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]　［54］戸祭台循環線または［54］西塙田経由戸祭・宝木団地線「[[宇都宮市立昭和小学校|昭和小学校]]前」下車、徒歩3分。 ;徒歩 * [[東武宇都宮駅]]から徒歩20分 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[宇都宮氏]] * [[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]] * [[戸祭山]] * [[曹洞宗]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html 曹洞宗戸祭山祥雲寺の公式ホームページ] {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しよううんし}} [[Category:栃木県の寺]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:宇都宮市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|||{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|||{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]]]] [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]]]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]]]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭山]]) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光山内]], which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光二荒山神社]] and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]], and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]], connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]], and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷台]]," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮美術館]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:うつのみや文化の森]]." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]], this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮駅]] West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:細谷車庫]], {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:山王団地]]: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:中戸祭]]," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第一グリーンヒル]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]], with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭田圃]] towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.

It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. == Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]], and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷台]]," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮美術館]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:うつのみや文化の森]]." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]], this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮駅]] West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:細谷車庫]], {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:山王団地]]: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:中戸祭]]," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第一グリーンヒル]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]], with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭田圃]] towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|||{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]]]] [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]]]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]]]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭山]]) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光山内]], which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光二荒山神社]] and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]], and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]], connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]], and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷台]]," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮美術館]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:うつのみや文化の森]]." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]], this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮駅]] West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第一グリーンヒル]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]], with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭田圃]] towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|||{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]]]] [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]]]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]]]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭山]]) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光山内]], which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光二荒山神社]] and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]], and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]], connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]], and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷台]]," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮美術館]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:うつのみや文化の森]]." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]], this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.

Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]]]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]]]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭山]]) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光山内]], which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光二荒山神社]] and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]], {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]], and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]], connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]], and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷台]]," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮美術館]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:うつのみや文化の森]]." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]], this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.

Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:細谷車庫]], {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:日光東照宮]], or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:山王団地]]: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:中戸祭]]," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:第一グリーンヒル]]" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]], with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭田圃]] towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
