completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.

It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Range|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.

Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]]]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]]]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭山]]) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Range|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:宇都宮丘陵]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:釜川]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山]]" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:大谷石]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:登録有形文化財]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:平和仏舎利塔]]" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]], {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:谷口山古墳]], {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]], and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:長岡百穴古墳]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]], where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]]" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:関東平野]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士山]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]]" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭台団地]]" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]][[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]]" (west slope). [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt.
