[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-qq]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * **[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]** * **Priests' Quarters** * **Sanmon Gate** * **Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)** * **Dōshō Kannon** * **Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)** * **Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site** ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * **Cemetery** ** **Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial** – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ** **The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * **Other Features** ** **Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)** ** **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** **Plum Grove** == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * **Weeping Cherry Tree** ** This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ** Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. revision.comment: removed [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]; added [[Category:Completion tier 5]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]][[ja:水道山]] Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt.
