[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |Image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]].== See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]].== See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also! == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}} [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]

The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb 11]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}}

Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=draft}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{draft categories|[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:return the see also]]|12=draft}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}}

{{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=draft}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{draft categories|[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] |12=draft}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}}

{{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=draft}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{draft categories|[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] |12=draft}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}}

Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{draft categories| |qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shoun-ji Utsunomiya}}

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市) up to rev 90021471
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 祥雲寺 |画像 = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
本堂 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]][[戸祭|東戸祭1-1-16]] |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|34|16|N|139|53|1|E}}| |山号 = [[戸祭山]] |宗派 = [[曹洞宗]] |本尊 = |創建年 = |開基 = 良訓和尚（開山） |正式名 = [[戸祭山]] 祥雲寺 |別称 = |札所等 = （祥雲寺観音霊場あり） |文化財 = [[しだれ桜]]（県指定[[天然記念物]]） |}} [[ファイル:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|山門]] '''祥雲寺'''（しょううんじ）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]][[戸祭|東戸祭一丁目]]にある[[曹洞宗]]の[[寺院]]である。山号は[[戸祭山]]。境内には祥雲寺観音霊場があり、当寺境内で[[西国三十三所|西国三十三所観音霊場]]の観音巡りができる。 == 境内 == * [[仏堂|本堂]] * 庫院 * [[山門]] * 伝燈閣 * 道正観音 * 羅漢渓 * 祥雲寺観音霊場（[[西国三十三所]]） * 墓地 ** 戸祭備中守高定 墓碑 – 戸祭高定は[[宇都宮氏]]第7代当主[[宇都宮景綱]]（[[1235年]]～[[1298年]]）の弟である[[宇都宮盛綱]]の子[[宇都宮泰貞]]の後裔で、この地に戸祭城を築城したとされる。 ** [[源義経]]の遺児[[千歳丸]]（[[中村朝定]]）の後裔、中村城主一族の[[中村氏 (下野国)]]の[[菩提寺]]。 * その他 ** 祥雲寺境内古墳（あまし塚古墳） ** [[しだれ桜]] ** 梅林 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|シダレザクラ]] * しだれ桜 ** [[江戸時代]]の[[明暦]]年間（[[1655年]]～[[1658年]]）に植えられたとされる古木で、樹齢300年といわれる。高さ約20m太さ5m、枝は東西約20m、南北約30mに広がる。 :: [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、[[栃木県]]より[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。 == アクセス == ;バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]][[宇都宮駅のバス乗り場|西口]]から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]　［54］戸祭台循環線または［54］西塙田経由戸祭・宝木団地線「[[宇都宮市立昭和小学校|昭和小学校]]前」下車、徒歩3分。 ;徒歩 * [[東武宇都宮駅]]から徒歩20分 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[宇都宮氏]] * [[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]] * [[戸祭山]] * [[曹洞宗]] * [[和尚塚 (宇都宮市)|和尚塚]] - 開山した良訓の墓 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html 曹洞宗戸祭山祥雲寺の公式ホームページ] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm 祥雲寺のしだれざくら] - [[栃木県総合教育センター]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しよううんし うつのみや}} [[Category:曹洞宗の寺院]] [[Category:宇都宮市の寺]] [[Category:栃木県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 祥雲寺 |画像 = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]

{{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan.
