Main Hall |Location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[Buddhist temple]] located in [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en}} ]] ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]]

The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ]] ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:宇都宮市の寺]]

Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} ]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ]] ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Sōtō temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tomatsuri-yama forks]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[en:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:栃木県指定天然記念物]]

The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[Edo period]], around the [[Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11591071]] Wikidata name: Shōun-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]]

Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[sn:Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[sn:hōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}}

Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}}

The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Main Hall |Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a [[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ([[:en:Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage]]) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], around the [[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a [[:en:Natural Monument of Japan|Prefectural Natural Monument]] by [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]]), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * [[:en:List of Buddhist temples in Japan|List of Buddhist temples in Japan]] * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, around the {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|en|Natural Monument of Japan|WD=Q122904442|lt=Prefectural Natural Monument}} by {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|en|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q10311993|lt=List of Buddhist temples in Japan}} * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi}}, [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ({{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, around the {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|en|Natural Monument of Japan|WD=Q122904442|lt=Prefectural Natural Monument}} by {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|en|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q10311993|lt=List of Buddhist temples in Japan}} * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ({{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, around the {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|en|Natural Monument of Japan|WD=Q122904442|lt=Prefectural Natural Monument}} by {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}}), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|en|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q10311993|lt=List of Buddhist temples in Japan}} * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ({{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, around the {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki|1=Meireki}} era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|en|Natural Monument of Japan|WD=Q122904442|lt=Prefectural Natural Monument|1=Natural Monument of Japan}} by {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|en|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q10311993|lt=List of Buddhist temples in Japan|1=List of Buddhist temples in Japan}} * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Sōtō|WD=Q749085|1=Sōtō}}|prefecture={{ill|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|1=Utsunomiya}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.571111|N|139.883611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Temple |Name = Shōun-ji |image = [[File:Shoun-ji (Utsunomiya).JPG|240px]]
Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-1-16 |LatitudeDegrees = 36 |LatitudeMinutes = 34 |LatitudeSeconds = 16 |LongitudeDegrees = 139 |LongitudeMinutes = 53 |LongitudeSeconds = 1 |Sect = [[Sōtō]] |Deity = |Founded = |Founder = Ryokun Oshō (First abbot) |FormalName = [[Tomatsuriyama]] Shōun-ji |OtherNames = |Pilgrimage = Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site |CulturalProperty = Weeping Cherry Tree (Prefectural Natural Monument) }} [[File:Shoun-ji Temple Gate, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Sanmon Gate]] '''Shōun-ji''' (祥雲寺) is a [[Sōtō]] {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name is [[Tomatsuriyama]]. The grounds include the Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site, where visitors can undertake a {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}. == Grounds == * '''[[Buddhist Hall|Main Hall]]''' * '''Priests' Quarters''' * '''Sanmon Gate''' * '''Dento-kaku (Traditional Lantern Pavilion)''' * '''Dōshō Kannon''' * '''Rakankei (Rakan Gorge)''' * '''Shōun-ji Kannon Pilgrimage Site''' ({{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q750721|lt=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}) * '''Cemetery''' ''' '''Tomasuri Bitchū-no-Kami Takasada Memorial''' – Tomasuri Takasada was a descendant of [[Utsunomiya clan]]'s 7th-generation head, [[Utsunomiya Kagetuna]] (1235–1298), and the son of [[Utsunomiya Moritsuna]]. He is said to have built Tomatsuri Castle on this site. ''' '''The descendants of [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]'s child, Chitosemaru (Nakamura Tomosada), and the Nakamura clan of Nakamura Castle, have their graves here. * '''Other Features''' ''' '''Shōun-ji Kofun (Amashi Mound Kofun)''' ''' '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' '''Plum Grove''' == Cultural Properties == [[File:Shidare-zakura of Shoun-ji Temple.jpg|thumb|Weeping Cherry Tree]] * '''Weeping Cherry Tree''' ''' This ancient cherry tree is believed to have been planted during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, around the {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki|1=Meireki}} era (1655–1658), and is estimated to be over 300 years old. It stands approximately 20 meters high, with a trunk circumference of 5 meters, and its branches extend 20 meters east-west and 30 meters north-south. :: On [[June 30, 1957]] (Shōwa 32), it was designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|en|Natural Monument of Japan|WD=Q122904442|lt=Prefectural Natural Monument|1=Natural Monument of Japan}} by {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}), West Exit: ''' Take the [[Kantō Bus]] No. 54, Tomatsuridai Loop Line, or No. 54, Nishi-Hanada via Tomatsuri and Takaramachi Line, and get off at "[[Utsunomiya Municipal Showa Elementary School|Showa Elementary School]]-mae," then walk 3 minutes. ; By Foot * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Station|Tōbu Utsunomiya Station]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|en|List of Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q10311993|lt=List of Buddhist temples in Japan|1=List of Buddhist temples in Japan}} * [[Utsunomiya clan]] * [[Hachimanyama Park (Utsunomiya)|Hachimanyama Park]] * [[Tomatsuriyama]] * [[Sōtō]] * [[Oshōzuka (Utsunomiya)|Oshōzuka]] – The tomb of Ryokun, the temple's founder == External Links == * [http://www.sho-un.jp/index.html Official Website of Sōtōshū Tomatsuriyama Shōun-ji] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/973.htm Shōun-ji Weeping Cherry Tree] - Tochigi Prefectural Comprehensive Education Center {{Buddhism-stub}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}} ''' ''' {{translated page|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|version=90021471|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:水道山]] [[ja:戸祭配水場]] [[ja:栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭山]] [[ja:日光山内]] [[ja:日光二荒山神社]] [[ja:日光東照宮]] [[ja:小倉山 (日光市)]] [[ja:愛宕山 (日光市)]] [[ja:茶臼山 (日光市)]] [[ja:浅間山 (日光市)]] [[ja:篠井富屋連峰]] [[ja:八幡山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:明神山 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮丘陵]] [[ja:田川 (利根川水系)]] [[ja:釜川]] [[ja:大谷石]] [[ja:戸祭配水場配水池]] [[ja:登録有形文化財]] [[ja:平和仏舎利塔]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:谷口山古墳]] [[ja:瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] [[ja:水道山瓦窯跡]] [[ja:薬師寺 (下野市)]] [[ja:上神主・茂原官衙遺跡]] [[ja:豊郷まほろばの道]] [[ja:関東平野]] [[ja:富士山]] [[ja:富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:戸祭台団地]] [[ja:戸祭グリーンヒル]] [[ja:長岡公園 (宇都宮市)]] [[ja:宇都宮環状道路]] [[ja:豊郷台]] [[ja:宇都宮美術館]] [[ja:うつのみや文化の森]] [[ja:帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス]] [[ja:宇都宮駅]] [[ja:関東自動車 (栃木県)]] [[ja:細谷車庫]] [[ja:山王団地]] [[ja:中戸祭]] [[ja:第二グリーンヒル南]] [[ja:第一グリーンヒル]] [[ja:戸祭田圃]] [[ja:田川]] [[ja:'''戸祭山''']] {{wikidata link|Q11591071}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Soto temples]] [[Category:Temples in Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Utsunomiya, Tochigi]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
